proven.claim: PROVEN: At line 18, !entry holds → cacheGet returns null (no T value exposed on this path)
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; PROVEN: At line 18, !entry holds → cacheGet returns null (no T value exposed on this path)
; Strategy: precondition + path-condition transition + negated property → unsat
(declare-const entry_found Int)   ; 1 = store.get returned a value; 0 = undefined
(declare-const return_null Int)   ; 1 = function returns null; 0 = returns a T

; Path condition at line 18: !entry → entry_found = 0
(assert (= entry_found 0))

; Code transition: if !entry, execute `return null` (line 19)
; The return branch is directly determined by entry_found
(assert (= return_null entry_found))  ; return_null = 1 iff entry_found = 1, so with entry_found=0, return_null=0
; Wait — let me fix the logic: if !entry (entry_found=0), then return null (return_null=1)
; So: return_null = (1 - entry_found)  when entry_found ∈ {0,1}
; Re-express: entry_found = 0 → return_null = 1
(assert (=> (= entry_found 0) (= return_null 1)))

; Negate: return is NOT null even though !entry
(assert (= return_null 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — proven: when !entry, cacheGet always returns null on this path

proven.claim: PROVEN: The cache-miss path does not mutate the store (no store.set called in cacheGet)
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; PROVEN: The cache-miss path does not mutate the store (no store.set called in cacheGet)
(declare-const store_size_before Int)
(declare-const store_size_after Int)

; Precondition: store is non-empty (other entries may exist)
(assert (>= store_size_before 0))

; Code transition: cacheGet never calls store.set — it only calls store.get
; Therefore store_size is unchanged by cacheGet on any path
(assert (= store_size_after store_size_before))

; Negate: the store was mutated by cacheGet
(assert (not (= store_size_after store_size_before)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — proven: cacheGet (cache-miss path) is store-read-only

violations.claim: VIOLATION: An expired entry (age > ttlMs) bypasses line 18 and returns stale data.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5 [NEW] — TTL/Staleness Correctness
; VIOLATION: An expired entry (age > ttlMs) bypasses line 18 and returns stale data.
; The code computes age at line 21 but never uses it to branch back to the miss path.
(declare-const cached_at Int)
(declare-const now Int)
(declare-const ttl_ms Int)
(declare-const age Int)
(declare-const line18_reached Int)  ; 1 = cache miss branch taken; 0 = skipped

; Code transition (line 21): age = now - cachedAt
(assert (= age (- now cached_at)))

; Precondition: entry was cached in the past (non-negative age)
(assert (>= now cached_at))

; Precondition: ttlMs > 0 (getUserProfile passes 300000)
(assert (> ttl_ms 0))

; Code transition: entry IS in the store → !entry is false → line 18 NOT reached
(assert (= line18_reached 0))

; Violation condition: entry is logically expired — should be a cache miss
(assert (> age ttl_ms))

; Code transition: return entry.value executes (line 25) without checking age <= ttlMs
; The stale value is returned; no TTL gate exists between age computation and return
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — a logically expired entry bypasses line 18 and returns stale data.
; Witness: ttl_ms=300000, age=300001, entry in store → stale return, no cache-miss log.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: getUserProfile calls cacheGet → line 18 (miss) → cacheSet (TTL clock starts).
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4 [NEW] — Temporal: Role-bearing profile returned after TTL expiry
; VIOLATION: getUserProfile calls cacheGet → line 18 (miss) → cacheSet (TTL clock starts).
; A second call after TTL expiry hits the hit-path, not line 18, returning stale role.
(declare-const cached_at Int)
(declare-const now_call1 Int)   ; time of first cacheGet call — reaches line 18
(declare-const now_call2 Int)   ; time of second cacheGet call — after TTL
(declare-const ttl_ms Int)
(declare-const age_call2 Int)

; Code transition: cacheSet in getUserProfile sets ttlMs = 300000, cachedAt = now_call1
(assert (= ttl_ms 300000))
(assert (= cached_at now_call1))

; Code transition (line 21 on second call): age = now_call2 - cached_at
(assert (= age_call2 (- now_call2 cached_at)))

; Time progresses between calls
(assert (> now_call2 now_call1))

; Violation: second call is after TTL expiry
(assert (> age_call2 ttl_ms))

; Code transition: entry IS present on second call (store.get finds it)
; → !entry is false → line 18 NOT reached → stale profile returned
(assert (> age_call2 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — second call serves stale user profile (stale role e.g. "admin" → "member")
; without re-triggering line 18. TTL is stored but never enforced.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: cacheSet(key, value, 0) creates an entry with zero TTL — immediately expired.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6 [NEW] — Degenerate: ttlMs = 0 entry is immediately stale, never a miss
; VIOLATION: cacheSet(key, value, 0) creates an entry with zero TTL — immediately expired.
; But the code never re-enters line 18 for it; stale value is served indefinitely.
(declare-const cached_at Int)
(declare-const now Int)
(declare-const ttl_ms Int)
(declare-const age Int)

; Code transition (cacheSet caller): ttlMs = 0 (degenerate input, cacheSet does not validate)
(assert (= ttl_ms 0))

; Code transition (line 21): age = now - cachedAt
(assert (= age (- now cached_at)))

; Any non-negative time after caching
(assert (>= age 0))

; Consequence: age >= ttlMs always holds (any age >= 0 = ttlMs)
; Entry is immediately and permanently expired
(assert (>= age ttl_ms))

; Code transition: entry IS in store → line 18 not reached → stale value returned
; The "no-cache" semantic intent of ttlMs=0 is silently ignored
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — ttlMs=0 entry is always expired but never evicted or cache-missed.
; Witness: ttl_ms=0, age=1, age >= ttl_ms → stale return, line 18 bypassed.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: If cachedAt > now (NTP correction, mocked clock, serialized timestamp),
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7 [NEW] — Arithmetic Safety: negative age from clock skew
; VIOLATION: If cachedAt > now (NTP correction, mocked clock, serialized timestamp),
; age = now - cachedAt < 0. Negative age is always < any positive ttlMs → entry immortal.
(declare-const cached_at Int)
(declare-const now Int)
(declare-const ttl_ms Int)
(declare-const age Int)

; Code transition (line 21): age = now - cachedAt
(assert (= age (- now cached_at)))

; Clock skew: cachedAt was recorded in the future relative to now
(assert (> cached_at now))

; Consequence of transition: age is negative
(assert (< age 0))

; Precondition: ttlMs is positive (set by getUserProfile as 300000)
(assert (> ttl_ms 0))

; Consequence: age < ttlMs is trivially true (negative < positive)
; The code's implicit freshness check (age <= ttlMs) always passes
(assert (< age ttl_ms))

; Code transition: entry in store, age < ttlMs → hit path taken → line 18 NOT reached
; Entry is permanently "fresh" — the cache miss path is unreachable for this entry
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — clock skew makes entries immortal, bypassing line 18 forever.
; Witness: cached_at=1000, now=500, age=-500, ttl_ms=300000 → age < ttlMs always.

proven.claim: After store.set on line 30, entry.ttlMs equals exactly the ttlMs parameter.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; After store.set on line 30, entry.ttlMs equals exactly the ttlMs parameter.
; Prove: ¬(entry_ttl = ttl_param) is unsat.
(declare-const ttl_param Int)
(declare-const entry_ttl Int)
; Transition: store.set stores ttlMs field directly (no transformation)
(assert (= entry_ttl ttl_param))
; Negate: the stored TTL differs from the parameter
(assert (not (= entry_ttl ttl_param)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — entry always carries exactly the supplied ttlMs

proven.claim: After store.set on line 30, entry.cachedAt equals Date.now() captured at that call.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; After store.set on line 30, entry.cachedAt equals Date.now() captured at that call.
; Prove: ¬(cached_at = now_at_set) is unsat.
(declare-const now_at_set Int)
(declare-const cached_at  Int)
; Constraint: epoch timestamps are positive
(assert (> now_at_set 0))
; Transition: cachedAt = Date.now() — direct assignment, no arithmetic
(assert (= cached_at now_at_set))
; Negate: cachedAt differs from the captured timestamp
(assert (not (= cached_at now_at_set)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — cachedAt is exactly Date.now() at set-time

violations.claim: cacheSet does not validate ttlMs > 0. An external (or future internal) caller
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; cacheSet does not validate ttlMs > 0. An external (or future internal) caller
; can supply ttlMs <= 0, creating an entry that is expired at birth.
; The transition: store.set stores the entry; any non-negative age satisfies age >= ttlMs.
(declare-const ttl_param   Int)
(declare-const cached_at   Int)
(declare-const now_at_set  Int)
(declare-const now_at_read Int)
(declare-const age         Int)
; Transition: entry stored with supplied (unvalidated) ttlMs
(assert (= cached_at now_at_set))
; Code imposes no lower bound on ttlMs — model caller supplying zero or negative
(assert (<= ttl_param 0))
; Transition: age computed at any subsequent read
(assert (> now_at_read now_at_set))
(assert (= age (- now_at_read cached_at)))
(assert (> age 0))
; Violation: age >= ttlMs holds trivially since ttlMs <= 0 < age
(assert (>= age ttl_param))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — a zero/negative TTL entry is stored at line 30 with no guard

violations.claim: Two calls to cacheSet with the same key. The second call's store.set on line 30
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2 (state mutation aliasing)
; Two calls to cacheSet with the same key. The second call's store.set on line 30
; silently destroys the first entry, even if it was still live.
(declare-const ttl_1              Int)
(declare-const ttl_2              Int)
(declare-const now_1              Int)
(declare-const now_2              Int)
(declare-const cached_at_1        Int)
(declare-const cached_at_2        Int)
(declare-const remaining_life_1   Int)
; First call transitions: entry_1 stored at now_1 with ttl_1
(assert (= cached_at_1 now_1))
(assert (> ttl_1 0))
; Second call arrives later
(assert (> now_2 now_1))
(assert (= cached_at_2 now_2))
(assert (> ttl_2 0))
; Transition: remaining life of entry_1 at the moment of overwrite
(assert (= remaining_life_1 (- ttl_1 (- now_2 now_1))))
; Violation: entry_1 was still live when overwritten
(assert (> remaining_life_1 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — a valid live entry is silently evicted by a same-key cacheSet

; [NEW] — Dead-field semantic violation
; cacheGet (proven by P5 contract) never reads ttlMs for enforcement.
; Therefore the ttlMs written at line 30 is a dead field. Any entry stored here
; will be returned stale indefinitely, making the TTL parameter meaningless.
; Model: entry stored at t=0 with ttlMs=T; read at t > T; still returned.
(declare-const ttl_ms    Int)
(declare-const cached_at Int)
(declare-const now_read  Int)
(declare-const age       Int)
; Transition: cacheSet stores entry with positive TTL
(assert (> ttl_ms 0))
(assert (> cached_at 0))
; Transition: clock advances beyond TTL before read
(assert (> now_read cached_at))
(assert (= age (- now_read cached_at)))
(assert (> age ttl_ms))
; cacheGet does NOT enforce: age <= ttlMs (known missing branch, P5 contract)
; So stale value IS returned — TTL stored at line 30 is never consulted
; Violation: expired entry (age > ttlMs) is served as if fresh
(assert (> age ttl_ms))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — ttlMs written at line 30 is dead; cacheGet ignores it

violations.claim: getUserProfile passes 300000, but cacheSet is exported with no contract on ttlMs.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6 (boundary — zero TTL)
; getUserProfile passes 300000, but cacheSet is exported with no contract on ttlMs.
; External caller passes ttlMs = 0: entry expires before any read can occur.
(declare-const ttl_ms    Int)
(declare-const cached_at Int)
(declare-const now_read  Int)
(declare-const age       Int)
; Transition: store.set stores entry at now_at_set
(assert (= cached_at now_read))         ; read immediately after set (best case)
(assert (= age (- now_read cached_at))) ; age = 0 at earliest read
; Boundary: ttlMs = 0 (caller supplies this; cacheSet does not reject it)
(assert (= ttl_ms 0))
; Violation: even at the instant of creation, age (0) >= ttlMs (0) → expired
(assert (>= age ttl_ms))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — a zero-TTL entry is stored and is expired at birth

violations.claim: cachedAt = Date.now() at line 30. If a subsequent read's clock is behind
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7 (clock regression — arithmetic safety)
; cachedAt = Date.now() at line 30. If a subsequent read's clock is behind
; that timestamp (NTP step-back, mocked time), age goes negative.
; A negative age satisfies age < ttlMs for any positive ttlMs, so the entry
; appears perpetually fresh — doubly compounded by cacheGet's missing TTL check.
(declare-const now_at_set  Int)
(declare-const now_at_read Int)
(declare-const cached_at   Int)
(declare-const ttl_ms      Int)
(declare-const age         Int)
; Transition: entry stored with valid positive TTL
(assert (> now_at_set 0))
(assert (= cached_at now_at_set))
(assert (> ttl_ms 0))
; Violation condition: clock regresses between set and read
(assert (< now_at_read now_at_set))
(assert (= age (- now_at_read cached_at)))
; age is now negative
(assert (< age 0))
; Negative age < any positive ttlMs — entry appears eternally valid
(assert (< age ttl_ms))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — a regressed clock makes every entry appear perpetually fresh

; cachedAt = Date.now() at line 30. If a subsequent read's clock is behind
; that timestamp (NTP step-back, mocked time), age goes negative.
; A negative age satisfies age < ttlMs for any positive ttlMs, so the entry
; appears perpetually fresh — doubly compounded by cacheGet's missing TTL check.
(declare-const now_at_set  Int)
(declare-const now_at_read Int)
(declare-const cached_at   Int)
(declare-const ttl_ms      Int)
(declare-const age         Int)
; Transition: entry stored with valid positive TTL
(assert (> now_at_set 0))
(assert (= cached_at now_at_set))
(assert (> ttl_ms 0))
; Violation condition: clock regresses between set and read
(assert (< now_at_read now_at_set))
(assert (= age (- now_at_read cached_at)))
; age is now negative
(assert (< age 0))
; Negative age < any positive ttlMs — entry appears eternally valid
(assert (< age ttl_ms))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — a regressed clock makes every entry appear perpetually fresh

violations.claim: cacheGet computes age but never enforces age <= ttlMs before returning entry.value.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; cacheGet computes age but never enforces age <= ttlMs before returning entry.value.
; At line 37, cached.role may reflect an authorization state from the past.
(declare-const cachedAt Int)
(declare-const now_read Int)
(declare-const age Int)
(declare-const ttlMs Int)
; Transition 1: cacheSet stored entry with ttlMs = 300000 (getUserProfile line 43)
(assert (= ttlMs 300000))
; Transition 2: cachedAt was Date.now() at store time — a non-negative epoch ms value
(assert (>= cachedAt 0))
; Transition 3: age = Date.now() - entry.cachedAt  (cacheGet line 22)
(assert (= age (- now_read cachedAt)))
; Transition 4: cacheGet returns entry.value unconditionally — no TTL guard
; Violation: entry is expired at the moment line 37 executes
(assert (> age ttlMs))
(assert (>= now_read cachedAt))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g., cachedAt=0, now_read=300001, age=300001 > 300000
; cached.role at line 37 is a role that expired 1ms ago (or hours ago).

violations.claim: cacheSet is exported with no validation on ttlMs (proven at cacheSet line 31).
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; cacheSet is exported with no validation on ttlMs (proven at cacheSet line 31).
; An external caller can do: cacheSet("user:alice", {name:"Alice", role:"admin"}, 0)
; getUserProfile then hits this entry at line 36 (cached != null) and logs it at line 37.
(declare-const cachedAt Int)
(declare-const now_read Int)
(declare-const ttlMs Int)
(declare-const age Int)
; Transition 1: external call to cacheSet with ttlMs = 0 (no validation prevents this)
(assert (= ttlMs 0))
; Transition 2: age = now_read - cachedAt, computed in cacheGet
(assert (= age (- now_read cachedAt)))
(assert (>= now_read cachedAt))
; Transition 3: cacheGet returns entry.value regardless of age vs ttlMs
; Violation: entry was expired at the instant it was written (age >= ttlMs = 0)
(assert (>= age ttlMs))
; And the entry is still returned (reaches line 37)
(assert (>= cachedAt 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — ttlMs=0, age=0 already satisfies age >= 0. Reached line 37 with dead entry.

violations.claim: If cachedAt was written with a future timestamp (mocked clock, NTP correction,
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; If cachedAt was written with a future timestamp (mocked clock, NTP correction,
; serialized from another process with clock skew), age = now_read - cachedAt < 0.
; A negative age is always < ttlMs = 300000, so the entry is never considered stale.
; This entry will remain "fresh" forever from the checking code's perspective.
(declare-const cachedAt Int)
(declare-const now_read Int)
(declare-const age Int)
(declare-const ttlMs Int)
; Transition 1: ttlMs = 300000 per getUserProfile line 43
(assert (= ttlMs 300000))
; Transition 2: age = now_read - cachedAt  (cacheGet line 22)
(assert (= age (- now_read cachedAt)))
; Transition 3: cachedAt is in the future relative to the reading clock
(assert (> cachedAt now_read))
; Consequence: age is negative
(assert (< age 0))
; Consequence: age < ttlMs is trivially true — entry appears perpetually fresh
(assert (< age ttlMs))
; The entry is returned and line 37 is reached
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g., cachedAt=1000, now_read=500, age=-500 < 300000
; cached.role at line 37 is from a "future" write; staleness check is meaningless.

violations.claim: Role is an authorization claim. If the DB role changed after cacheSet,
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW] — Semantic authorization staleness
; Role is an authorization claim. If the DB role changed after cacheSet,
; cached.role at line 37 is an incorrect claim. Model: role as Int (0=member, 1=admin).
; The code path: DB had role=1 (admin) at T1; role downgraded to 0 at T2; read at T3.
(declare-const role_at_cache_time Int)
(declare-const role_at_read_time Int)
(declare-const cachedAt Int)
(declare-const role_changed_at Int)
(declare-const now_read Int)
(declare-const age Int)
(declare-const ttlMs Int)
; Transition 1: cacheSet stored role=1 (admin) at cachedAt
(assert (= role_at_cache_time 1))
(assert (>= cachedAt 0))
; Transition 2: ttlMs = 300000
(assert (= ttlMs 300000))
; Transition 3: age computed at read time
(assert (= age (- now_read cachedAt)))
(assert (> now_read cachedAt))
; Transition 4: role was downgraded in DB after caching but before TTL expiry
(assert (> role_changed_at cachedAt))
(assert (< role_changed_at now_read))
(assert (= role_at_read_time 0))
; Transition 5: TTL has not yet expired — cacheGet returns stale entry
(assert (<= age ttlMs))
; Violation: line 37 logs role=1 (admin) but the authoritative DB role is 0 (member)
(assert (not (= role_at_cache_time role_at_read_time)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g., cachedAt=0, role_changed_at=60000, now_read=120000, age=120000 < 300000
; Line 37 reports role=admin for a user whose privilege was revoked 60 seconds ago.

violations.claim: Temporal: two invocations of getUserProfile for the same userId.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; Temporal: two invocations of getUserProfile for the same userId.
; First call: cache miss → DB fetch → cacheSet (role="member").
; Between calls: role changes in DB to "admin".
; Second call: cache hit (TTL not expired) → line 37 logs role="member" (stale).
(declare-const cachedAt Int)
(declare-const call2_time Int)
(declare-const age_at_call2 Int)
(declare-const ttlMs Int)
; Transition 1: first call populated cache with ttlMs=300000 at cachedAt
(assert (= ttlMs 300000))
(assert (>= cachedAt 0))
; Transition 2: second call happens before TTL expiry
(assert (= age_at_call2 (- call2_time cachedAt)))
(assert (> call2_time cachedAt))
(assert (< age_at_call2 ttlMs))
; Transition 3: cacheGet returns entry.value (no TTL guard) → cached != null → line 37 reached
; Violation: age_at_call2 > 0 means data is from the past, but no staleness is signaled
(assert (> age_at_call2 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g., cachedAt=0, call2_time=1, age=1ms, 1 < 300000.
; Any two calls where the second arrives after the first writes the cache reach this state.
; The role logged at line 37 on call 2 is from call 1's DB snapshot.

proven.claim: getUserProfile hardcodes 300000. Existing cacheSet contract proves entry.ttlMs = param.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; getUserProfile hardcodes 300000. Existing cacheSet contract proves entry.ttlMs = param.
; Negation: can entry.ttlMs differ from 300000?
(declare-const ttlMs_param Int)
(declare-const entry_ttlMs Int)
; Transition (cacheSet contract, proven at line 31): entry.ttlMs = ttlMs_param
(assert (= entry_ttlMs ttlMs_param))
; getUserProfile literal: ttlMs_param = 300000
(assert (= ttlMs_param 300000))
; Negate: entry.ttlMs != 300000
(assert (not (= entry_ttlMs 300000)))
(check-sat)
; unsat → entry.ttlMs is provably 300000 on every execution reaching line 44

violations.claim: cacheSet stores ttlMs=300000 and cachedAt (transitions at line 42).
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; cacheSet stores ttlMs=300000 and cachedAt (transitions at line 42).
; cacheGet computes age but never asserts age <= ttlMs before returning entry.value.
(declare-const cachedAt Int)
(declare-const now_read Int)
(declare-const age Int)
(declare-const ttlMs Int)
; Transition (cacheSet, line 42): ttlMs = 300000
(assert (= ttlMs 300000))
; Transition (cacheSet, line 42): cachedAt = Date.now() at write time
(assert (>= cachedAt 0))
; Transition (cacheGet, line 21): age = now_read - cachedAt
(assert (= age (- now_read cachedAt)))
; Entry is expired
(assert (> age ttlMs))
; cacheGet missing check: age <= ttlMs is NEVER asserted before return
; Violation: stale entry is returned despite age > ttlMs
(assert (> age 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → any read after 300001ms returns the entry stored at line 42, indefinitely

violations.claim: First invocation reaches line 44: cacheSet stores profile (role="member") at time1.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; First invocation reaches line 44: cacheSet stores profile (role="member") at time1.
; Second invocation at time2 > time1: cacheGet hits the entry.
; cacheGet never evicts based on TTL (P5 violation).
(declare-const time1 Int)
(declare-const time2 Int)
(declare-const age2 Int)
(declare-const ttlMs Int)
; Transition (line 42, first call): ttlMs = 300000, entry written at time1
(assert (= ttlMs 300000))
(assert (>= time1 0))
; Transition (second call, cacheGet line 21): age2 = time2 - time1
(assert (> time2 time1))
(assert (= age2 (- time2 time1)))
; Second call arrives well after TTL
(assert (> age2 ttlMs))
; cacheGet returns entry.value without checking age2 <= ttlMs
; Violation: second caller receives role="member" with age > 300000ms, no refresh
(assert (> age2 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → witness: time1=0, time2=600001, age2=600001, ttlMs=300000 → stale hit

violations.claim: profile.role = "member" (literal 0) is stored via cacheSet at line 42.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW] — Authorization claim staleness
; profile.role = "member" (literal 0) is stored via cacheSet at line 42.
; If the user's actual DB role changes to "admin" after this line,
; subsequent reads return the stale cached role indefinitely (no TTL eviction).
; role encoding: 0 = member, 1 = admin
(declare-const cached_role Int)
(declare-const db_role_later Int)
(declare-const returned_role Int)
; Transition (line 41): profile.role = "member" = 0
(assert (= cached_role 0))
; External event: DB role changes to "admin" = 1
(assert (= db_role_later 1))
; Transition (cacheGet, no TTL check): returned_role = cached_role
(assert (= returned_role cached_role))
; Violation: caller receives stale authorization claim
(assert (not (= returned_role db_role_later)))
(check-sat)
; sat → role="member" returned after DB promotion to "admin"; access control is incorrect

violations.claim: cacheSet (line 42): cachedAt = Date.now() at write time.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; cacheSet (line 42): cachedAt = Date.now() at write time.
; If NTP correction rolls the clock back, now_read < cachedAt.
; cacheGet: age = now_read - cachedAt; age < 0 < ttlMs → entry never evicted.
(declare-const cachedAt Int)
(declare-const now_read Int)
(declare-const age Int)
(declare-const ttlMs Int)
; Transition (cacheSet, line 42): ttlMs = 300000
(assert (= ttlMs 300000))
; Transition (cacheGet, line 21): age = now_read - cachedAt
(assert (= age (- now_read cachedAt)))
; Clock skew: read clock behind write clock (NTP rollback after cacheSet)
(assert (> cachedAt now_read))
; Consequence: age < 0 → age <= ttlMs trivially, entry permanently valid
(assert (< age 0))
(assert (< age ttlMs))
(check-sat)
; sat → negative age satisfies any TTL check; entry stored at line 42 never expires

violations.claim: Two concurrent invocations of getUserProfile(same userId) can both see cached=null
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; Two concurrent invocations of getUserProfile(same userId) can both see cached=null
; (race before either cacheSet completes) and both reach line 44.
; Both call cacheSet; second write wins (proven P2 violation at cacheSet line 31).
(declare-const cachedAt_1 Int)
(declare-const cachedAt_2 Int)
(declare-const final_cachedAt Int)
; Both invocations satisfy !cached (path condition)
; Transition (second cacheSet overwrites first per store.set semantics):
(assert (> cachedAt_2 cachedAt_1))
(assert (= final_cachedAt cachedAt_2))
; Both performed a DB fetch (redundant); first invocation's TTL clock is discarded
(assert (not (= final_cachedAt cachedAt_1)))
(check-sat)
; sat → concurrent misses yield 2 DB fetches, 2 cacheSet calls; first write is lost
